message:
Add agent-sweep utility functions

- Add has_uncommitted_changes() for dirty repo detection
- Add repo_spec_to_path() for converting repo specs to paths
- Add load_all_repos() for loading repo list from config

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-obd9","title":"Create comprehensive test suite for review feature","description":"Comprehensive tests for the review feature.

Unit Tests (scripts/test_unit_review.sh):
- test_graphql_batching: verify query generation and parsing
- test_priority_scoring: verify score calculation for all components
- test_plan_validation: verify schema validation catches errors
- test_state_persistence: verify atomic writes and locking
- test_question_detection: verify all three wait reasons

Integration Tests (scripts/test_e2e_review.sh):
- test_dry_run_discovery: verify discovery without sessions
- test_plan_mode_no_mutations: verify gh mutations blocked
- test_worktree_isolation: verify main repo unchanged
- test_resume_from_checkpoint: verify resume works
- test_quality_gates: verify tests run before push

Test Fixtures (test/fixtures/):
- claude_stream/*.ndjson: stream-json examples
- gh/*.json: GraphQL and REST responses
- plans/*.json: review plan examples

Golden Artifacts:
- Expected plan output for fixture repos
- Comparison ignores timestamps

Mocking:
- Mock gh command for unit tests
- Mock claude for integration tests

-{"id":"bd-okbr","title":"Implement core utility functions for agent-sweep","description":"Implements core utility functions used across agent-sweep implementation.

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Provide shared utility functions that are used by multiple components of agent-sweep.

## Functions to Implement

### has_uncommitted_changes()
```bash
# Check if repo has uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged)
# Args: $1=repo_path
# Returns: 0 if dirty, 1 if clean
has_uncommitted_changes() {
    local repo_path="$1"

    # Check for any changes (staged, unstaged, or untracked)
    if [[ -n "$(git -C "$repo_path" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
        return 0  # Has changes
    fi
    return 1  # Clean
}
```

### repo_spec_to_path()
```bash
# Convert repo spec (owner/repo[@branch]) to local filesystem path
# Args: $1=repo_spec
# Returns: path via stdout
repo_spec_to_path() {
    local repo_spec="$1"

    # Strip optional @branch suffix
    local repo="${repo_spec%%@*}"

    # Handle owner/repo format
    local repo_name="${repo##*/}"

    # Use configured projects directory
    local projects_dir="${RU_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/projects}"

    echo "${projects_dir}/${repo_name}"
}
```

### load_all_repos()
```bash
# Load all repos from config files into array
# Args: $1=array_name_ref
load_all_repos() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    repos_ref=()

    local config_dir="${RU_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/ru}"
    local repos_d="${config_dir}/repos.d"

    # Load from each file in repos.d
    if [[ -d "$repos_d" ]]; then
        for file in "$repos_d"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$file" ]] || continue
            while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
                # Skip comments and empty lines
                [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
                [[ -z "${line// }" ]] && continue
                repos_ref+=("$line")
            done < "$file"
        done
    fi
}
```

### sanitize_session_name()
```bash
# Sanitize repo name for use as tmux session name
# Args: $1=repo_name
sanitize_session_name() {
    local name="$1"
    # Replace non-alphanumeric chars with underscore
    echo "${name//[^a-zA-Z0-9_]/_}"
}
```

### strip_ansi()
```bash
# Strip ANSI escape codes from string
# Useful for parsing pane output
strip_ansi() {
    # Use sed to remove ANSI escape sequences
    sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]//g'
}
```

### get_file_size_mb()
```bash
# Get file size in MB (for display)
# Args: $1=file_path
get_file_size_mb() {
    local file="$1"
    local size_bytes
    size_bytes=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    echo "scale=1; $size_bytes / 1048576" | bc
}
```

### array_contains()
```bash
# Check if array contains element
# Args: $1=array_name, $2=element
array_contains() {
    local -n arr=$1
    local elem="$2"
    for item in "${arr[@]}"; do
        [[ "$item" == "$elem" ]] && return 0
    done
    return 1
}
```

### get_repo_name()
```bash
# Extract repo name from path or spec
# Args: $1=path_or_spec
get_repo_name() {
    local input="$1"
    # Handle both /path/to/repo and owner/repo formats
    basename "${input%%@*}"
}
```

### ensure_directory()
```bash
# Create directory if it doesn't exist
# Args: $1=dir_path
ensure_directory() {
    local dir="$1"
    [[ -d "$dir" ]] || mkdir -p "$dir"
}
```

## Location
These functions should be defined early in the ru script, in a utilities section, so they're available to all agent-sweep functions.

## Integration Points
- Used by: cmd_agent_sweep() for loading repos
- Used by: run_sequential/parallel_sweep() for path resolution
- Used by: session naming for tmux
- Used by: plan extraction for ANSI stripping
- Used by: file size checking for validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] has_uncommitted_changes() correctly detects all change types
- [ ] repo_spec_to_path() handles owner/repo and @branch formats
- [ ] load_all_repos() loads from all files in repos.d
- [ ] sanitize_session_name() produces valid tmux session names
- [ ] strip_ansi() removes all escape sequences
- [ ] All functions work on both Linux and macOS","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:37:49.376668698-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:07:45.66757724-05:00"}

```bash
# Check if repo has uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged)

has_uncommitted_changes() {
```

### repo_spec_to_path()

### load_all_repos()
```bash
# Load all repos from config files into array

load_all_repos() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
```

 {"id":"bd-p0qh","title":"Audit fixes: doctor flock check + self-update version fallback","description":"Fresh-eyes audit findings:\n- ru doctor didn't check flock (required for review locking).\n- ru self-update had brittle tag_name parsing and would fail on API rate limits/response changes; should fall back to /releases/latest redirect similar to installer.\n- install.sh curl used -f and dropped API JSON error message; keep body for better diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T10:38:34.991745956-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T10:38:48.55981594-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T10:38:48.55981594-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented doctor flock checks, self-update redirect fallback, and installer API body retention"}
 {"id":"bd-p2ip","title":"Implement review lock mechanism (flock-based)","description":"# Task: Implement Review Lock Mechanism

## Purpose
Prevent concurrent `ru review` runs which would cause chaos with overlapping worktrees, duplicate API calls, and conflicting state updates.

## Implementation Details

### Lock File Location
```bash
REVIEW_LOCK_FILE="$RU_STATE_DIR/review.lock"
```

### acquire_review_lock()
```bash
acquire_review_lock() {
    mkdir -p "$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Open fd 200 for locking
    exec 200>"$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE"
    
    # Try non-blocking lock
    if ! flock -n 200; then
        # Check who holds it
        local holder_info=""
        if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
            holder_info=$(cat "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info")
        fi
        log_error "Another review session is active"
        [[ -n "$holder_info" ]] && log_error "Started: $holder_info"
        log_info "Use 'ru review --status' to check, or wait for completion"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Write lock info
    cat > "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" << EOF
{
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "pid": $$,
  "mode": "$mode"
}
EOF
    
    return 0
}
```

### release_review_lock()
```bash
release_review_lock() {
    # Remove info file
    rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
    
    # Release flock
    flock -u 200 2>/dev/null || true
}
```

### Trap Handler Integration
```bash
# In cmd_review(), set up trap
trap 'release_review_lock; exit 130' INT TERM

# Ensure cleanup on any exit
trap_cleanup_review() {
    release_review_lock
    cleanup_stale_worktrees
}
trap trap_cleanup_review EXIT
```

### Stale Lock Detection
```bash
check_stale_lock() {
    if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
        local lock_pid
        lock_pid=$(jq -r '.pid' "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" 2>/dev/null)
        
        # Check if process still exists
        if [[ -n "$lock_pid" ]] && ! kill -0 "$lock_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Found stale lock from dead process $lock_pid"
            rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
            return 0  # Lock is stale
        fi
    fi
    return 1  # Lock is valid or doesn't exist
}
```

## Why flock?
- Atomic lock acquisition
- Automatically released on process exit (even crash)
- No race conditions
- Works on Linux, macOS, BSD
- Already used successfully in parallel sync

## Edge Cases
- Process killed with SIGKILL: Lock released by kernel, but .info file remains
- Machine reboot: Lock file and .info persist but are stale
- Multiple users: Each user has own state dir, so no conflict

## Testing
- Verify lock prevents second ru review
- Verify lock released on normal exit
- Verify lock released on Ctrl+C
- Verify stale lock detected after kill -9
- Verify lock info shows useful details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Only one ru review can run at a time
- [ ] Lock info shows run_id, start time, PID
- [ ] Lock released on all exit paths
- [ ] Stale locks from crashed processes handled","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.209434378-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented full flock-based review lock mechanism with JSON info file (run_id, started_at, pid, mode). Added check_stale_lock() for dead process detection. Proper trap handlers for INT/TERM/EXIT. All acceptance criteria met: single concurrent review, detailed lock info, cleanup on all exit paths, stale lock detection.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p2ip","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.831071716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -327,6 +327,108 @@ ensure_dir() {
     fi
 }
 
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# AGENT-SWEEP UTILITY FUNCTIONS
+# Shared utilities used by agent-sweep command and related functions.
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+# Check if repo has uncommitted changes (staged, unstaged, or untracked)
+# Usage: has_uncommitted_changes /path/to/repo
+# Returns: 0 if dirty (has changes), 1 if clean
+has_uncommitted_changes() {
+    local repo_path="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$repo_path" ]] && return 1
+    [[ -n "$(git -C "$repo_path" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)" ]]
+}
+
+# Convert repo spec (owner/repo[@branch]) to local filesystem path
+# Usage: repo_spec_to_path "owner/repo@branch"
+# Outputs: path to stdout
+repo_spec_to_path() {
+    local repo_spec="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$repo_spec" ]] && return 1
+
+    # Strip optional @branch suffix
+    local repo="${repo_spec%%@*}"
+    # Extract repo name (last component)
+    local repo_name="${repo##*/}"
+    # Use configured projects directory
+    local projects_dir="${RU_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/projects}"
+
+    echo "${projects_dir}/${repo_name}"
+}
+
+# Load all repos from config files into an array
+# Usage: load_all_repos array_name
+# Populates the named array with repo specs
+load_all_repos() {
+    local -n repos_ref=$1
+    repos_ref=()
+
+    local config_dir="${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/ru"
+    local repos_d="${config_dir}/repos.d"
+
+    # Load from each file in repos.d
+    if [[ -d "$repos_d" ]]; then
+        local file
+        for file in "$repos_d"/*.txt; do
+            [[ -f "$file" ]] || continue
+            local line
+            while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
+                # Skip comments and empty lines
+                [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
+                [[ -z "${line// }" ]] && continue
+                repos_ref+=("$line")
+            done < "$file"
+        done
+    fi
+}
+
+# Strip ANSI escape codes from input (useful for parsing pane output)
+# Usage: echo "$text" | strip_ansi
+strip_ansi() {
+    sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]//g'
+}
+
+# Get file size in MB (for display/validation)
+# Usage: get_file_size_mb /path/to/file
+# Outputs: size in MB (one decimal place)
+get_file_size_mb() {
+    local file="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$file" || ! -f "$file" ]] && { echo "0"; return; }
+
+    local size_bytes
+    # Try GNU stat first, fall back to BSD stat
+    size_bytes=$(stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
+
+    # Use awk for portability (bc may not be available)
+    awk "BEGIN { printf \"%.1f\", $size_bytes / 1048576 }"
+}
+
+# Check if array contains an element
+# Usage: array_contains array_name "element"
+# Returns: 0 if found, 1 if not found
+array_contains() {
+    local -n arr=$1
+    local elem="$2"
+    local item
+    for item in "${arr[@]}"; do
+        [[ "$item" == "$elem" ]] && return 0
+    done
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Extract repo name from path or spec
+# Usage: get_repo_name "/path/to/repo" or "owner/repo@branch"
+# Outputs: repo name to stdout
+get_repo_name() {
+    local input="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$input" ]] && return 1
+    # Handle both /path/to/repo and owner/repo formats
+    # Strip @branch suffix if present
+    basename "${input%%@*}"
+}
+
 # mktemp compatibility: BSD (macOS) mktemp requires a template or -t.
 mktemp_file() {
     local tmp
